refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall provide worksite area analysis including terrain representation.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR combined multiple obligations: terrain, obstacles, and work-zone representation. To ensure atomicity and clarity, these are separated into distinct sub-FRs. This refined FR focuses solely on terrain representation, which is a fundamental aspect of worksite area analysis and remains solution-neutral.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Verify that the system can generate and provide a representation of the terrain within the worksite area, independent of obstacle or work-zone data.
sub_frs: The NGHE solution shall provide worksite area analysis including terrain representation.
The NGHE solution shall provide worksite area analysis including obstacle representation.
The NGHE solution shall provide worksite area analysis including work-zone representation.

candidate_dps: Generate a digital terrain model representing the physical features of the worksite
Create a semantic map annotating terrain with relevant site features and safety zones
Develop a modular worksite model combining terrain data with operational policy overlays
Produce an abstracted spatial representation supporting dynamic updates and integration with planning modules
selected_dp.selected_dp: Develop a modular worksite model combining terrain data with operational policy overlays

potential_sources: Integration of terrain data and operational policies may create dependencies if not modularized
coupling_check.notes: By modularizing terrain representation and policy overlays as separate but interfaced components within the worksite model, independence is maintained and coupling minimized.

assumptions_risks: Assumes availability of accurate terrain data and site feature definitions
Risk that operational policies may change frequently requiring flexible model updates
Assumes planning and execution functions can consume the modular worksite model format
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Clarify that the worksite model should be modular and extensible to accommodate updates in terrain data and operational policies independently, ensuring low coupling and ease of integration with planning/execution functions.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The NGHE Solution module encompasses the integration and processing of environmental and operational data to create a modular worksite model that combines terrain data with operational policy overlays.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Receives terrain and sensor data relevant to the worksite environment.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Receives operational policies and task directives to overlay on the terrain model.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Facilitates data exchange with other system modules and external data sources.

module_containment_scores.notes: High cohesion as the module is designed to handle environmental and operational data integration; low diffusion since most data processing is internal.
Vehicle module primarily focuses on vehicle control and dynamics; containing the worksite model here would cause significant diffusion to other modules.
Teleoperation involves remote control and monitoring; partial relevance but containing the DP here would cause diffusion of terrain and policy data.
Safety Services focus on alerts and safety monitoring; worksite modeling is outside its primary scope, leading to poor containment and high diffusion.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The NGHE Solution module is the most appropriate boundary for containing the DP as it naturally integrates terrain and operational policy data to build the worksite model, minimizing diffusion and maximizing cohesion.
containment_risks: Potential dependency on real-time data feeds may require careful interface management to avoid latency issues.
Changes in operational policies might require frequent updates to the module, impacting stability.
Integration complexity with other modules like Vehicle and Teleoperation could increase coupling if interfaces are not well defined.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The DP logically addresses the FR by specifying a modular worksite model that combines terrain data with operational policy overlays, directly fulfilling the requirement to provide worksite area analysis including terrain representation.

diffusion_risks: Potential dependency on real-time data feeds may require careful interface management to avoid latency issues.
Changes in operational policies might require frequent updates to the module, impacting stability.
Integration complexity with other modules like Vehicle and Teleoperation could increase coupling if interfaces are not well defined.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: The NGHE Solution module shows high cohesion and low diffusion scores, indicating good containment of the DP within a single module. Other modules have low containment and high diffusion scores, supporting the choice of NGHE Solution as the appropriate IM boundary.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification is feasible as the NGHE Solution module receives telemetry and tasking inputs necessary to generate the worksite model, which can be validated against acceptance criteria requiring availability of the model to planning/execution functions.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: The ownership candidate and module scope clearly allocate the DP to the NGHE Solution module, with well-defined external interfaces supporting data inputs and integration.

recommended_revisions: Explicitly define interface contracts and data update frequencies to mitigate latency and integration risks.
Establish a change management process for operational policy updates to maintain module stability.
Consider monitoring coupling metrics between NGHE Solution and related modules to detect and manage integration complexity.

decision.justification: All gates (FR->DP, DP->IM, FR->IM) have passed successfully, indicating that the current level of decomposition sufficiently addresses the functional requirement with a well-defined design parameter and implementation module. The NGHE Solution module demonstrates high cohesion and low diffusion, with clear ownership and feasible verification. Although there are some integration and latency risks, these are manageable through recommended revisions rather than further decomposition. Given the high safety criticality, maintaining a clear and stable module boundary is preferable to avoid complexity and potential errors from deeper decomposition.

revise.why: To explicitly define interface contracts and data update frequencies, establish change management for operational policy updates, and monitor coupling metrics to mitigate integration and latency risks.

assumptions: The NGHE Solution module can handle the complexity of the worksite model generation within a single module.
Integration with other modules can be managed through well-defined interfaces and change processes.
open_risks: Potential latency issues from real-time data feeds.
Stability risks due to frequent operational policy updates.
Integration complexity increasing coupling with other modules.
evidence_needed: Verification results confirming the NGHE Solution module meets acceptance criteria for worksite model availability.
Metrics on interface performance and coupling with related modules.
Documentation of change management processes for operational policies.
